basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Sheba — a Meccan sūra of fifty-four verses — opens on total praise: to God belongs whatever is in the heavens and on the earth, and to Him be praise in the Hereafter, the Wise, the All-Aware, from whom not even an atom's weight escapes His knowledge. Qushayrī sees in it at once the One who from all eternity praises Himself, the sole Existent, the Adored in the pre-eternities, the Goal of every quest. The sūra unfolds the favors granted to David and Solomon: the mountains and birds echoing back the praise, the iron made soft, the wind subjected, carrying a month's march in the morning and a month in the evening, the spring of molten copper that flows, the jinn at work. Then the great call: “Work in gratitude, O family of David” — and this word that runs through the whole sūra: “Few of My servants are truly grateful,” for most take the bounty of God, then ascribe its merit to secondary causes, and thank other than Him. There follows the account of the people of Sheba with their two gardens, filled with abundance and security, commanded to do the easiest of things — to be patient in ease and give thanks — and who turned away; the flood of the dam (sayl al-ʿarim) changed their orchards into bitter thickets, and their name became a proverb of dispersal: “in their story are signs for every being patient in trial, grateful in grace.” Then come the powerlessness of the false gods, who possess not an atom's weight, the universal mission of the Messenger to all mankind, and the final proclamation: “My Lord casts the Truth, the Knower of the unseen” — for falsehood only vanishes the more with the passing of days, and the Truth only grows in strength and splendor.
themes: the favors given to David and Solomon
gratitude and the few who are grateful
the people of Sheba and the flood of the dam
the powerlessness of the false gods
the Truth cast and the universal mission

versets.ar: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ وَهُوَ الْحَكِيمُ الْخَبِيرُ
versets.tr: al-ḥamdu li-llāhi lladhī lahu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-lahu l-ḥamdu fī l-ākhirati wa-huwa l-ḥakīmu l-khabīru
versets.trad: Praise be to God, to whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth! And to Him be praise in the Hereafter! He is the Wise, the All-Aware.
versets.commentaire: The sūra opens with the praise God renders to Himself: His praise of Himself is a testimony to His majesty and His worthiness of the attributes of His glory. Qushayrī: from all eternity He is the One who praises Himself, the Praised, the sole Existent, the Adored in the pre-eternities, the Goal of every quest. “To whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth”: kingship admits no partner — there is no king but God. And His praise endures in the Hereafter: the praise of those whom He has set free, whom He has immersed in grace.

mots.def: the laud due to God alone.

mots.sens: to Him belongs what is in the heavens

mots.racine: ح ك م / خ ب ر
mots.def: two Names: perfect wisdom and intimate knowledge of all.

versets.ar: يَعْلَمُ مَا يَلِجُ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَمَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا وَمَا يَنْزِلُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ وَمَا يَعْرُجُ فِيهَا وَهُوَ الرَّحِيمُ الْغَفُورُ
versets.tr: yaʿlamu mā yaliju fī l-arḍi wa-mā yakhruju minhā wa-mā yanzilu mina s-samāʾi wa-mā yaʿruju fīhā wa-huwa r-raḥīmu l-ghafūru
versets.trad: He knows what enters the earth and what comes forth from it, what descends from the sky and what ascends into it. He is the Compassionate, the Forgiving.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds what the divine knowledge embraces: what enters the earth — the seed beneath the soil, the water that seeps into it, and the dead who are buried within it; what comes forth from it — the plant, the flowers, and the dead who are raised. Likewise what descends from the sky — the rain, the angel, the blessing, the provision, the decree; and what ascends into it — the scrolls of deeds, the needs of men, and the lofty aspirations of the saints. Then He seals it with two Names of gentleness: the Compassionate toward His servants, the Forgiver of all sinners.

mots.racine: ر ح م / غ ف ر

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَا تَأْتِينَا السَّاعَةُ ۖ قُلْ بَلَىٰ وَرَبِّي لَتَأْتِيَنَّكُمْ عَالِمِ الْغَيْبِ ۖ لَا يَعْزُبُ عَنْهُ مِثْقَالُ ذَرَّةٍ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَلَا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا أَصْغَرُ مِنْ ذَٰلِكَ وَلَا أَكْبَرُ إِلَّا فِي كِتَابٍ مُبِينٍ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna kafarū lā taʾtīnā s-sāʿatu qul balā wa-rabbī la-taʾtiyannakum ʿālimi l-ghaybi lā yaʿzubu ʿanhu mithqālu dharratin fī s-samāwāti wa-lā fī l-arḍi wa-lā aṣgharu min dhālika wa-lā akbaru illā fī kitābin mubīnin
versets.trad: Those who disbelieve say: “The Hour will not come upon us.” Say: “Indeed it will! By my Lord, it will surely come upon you!” He is the Knower of the unseen. Not even an atom's weight escapes Him, neither anything smaller nor anything greater, whether in the heavens or on the earth, but it is in a clear Book,
versets.commentaire: The Qurʾān repeats the denial the disbelievers raise against the Hour, their improbability, and the answer made to them. Qushayrī: God informs of His prior knowledge concerning them: nothing of what He knows escapes His knowledge. He thereby affirms that His knowledge embraces all things and encompasses them entirely; for were He to be ignorant of one, it would be a deficiency, and were a single known thing to escape Him, His power would be diminished — yet deficiency, under any attribute whatever, is inadmissible in Him.

mots.racine: ث ق ل / ذ ر ر

versets.ar: لِيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ
versets.tr: li-yajziya lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti ulāʾika lahum maghfiratun wa-rizqun karīmun
versets.trad: that He may reward those who believe and do righteous deeds. For them is forgiveness and a generous provision.
versets.commentaire: Perfect knowledge issues in the justice of recompense. Qushayrī: the doers of good He rewards with graces that ceaselessly follow one another; the disbelievers He requites for their disbelief with unbroken torments. “Forgiveness” precedes “generous provision”: first the effacing of the stain, then the bestowal of bounty — the very order of grace, which purifies before it fills.

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ك ر م

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ سَعَوْا فِي آيَاتِنَا مُعَاجِزِينَ أُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مِّن رِّجْزٍ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna saʿaw fī āyātinā muʿājizīna ulāʾika lahum ʿadhābun min rijzin alīmun
versets.trad: But those who strive against Our Signs, seeking to thwart them, for them is a torment of painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: Set against the believers who are filled with grace are those who strive against the Signs, imagining they can render them powerless. Qushayrī sets the two destinies in a single sentence: to the doers of good, graces that follow on; to the recalcitrant, torments that never cease. To wish to “thwart” God's Signs is but a presumption that turns back upon itself as torment — the punishment of opposition to the verses of God.

mots.def: to seek to frustrate, to defy.

mots.racine: ر ج ز / أ ل م

versets.ar: وَيَرَى الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ الَّذِي أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ مِن رَّبِّكَ هُوَ الْحَقَّ وَيَهْدِي إِلَىٰ صِرَاطِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَمِيدِ
versets.tr: wa-yarā lladhīna ūtū l-ʿilma lladhī unzila ilayka min rabbika huwa l-ḥaqqa wa-yahdī ilā ṣirāṭi l-ʿazīzi l-ḥamīdi
versets.trad: And those who have been given knowledge see that what has been sent down to you from your Lord is the Truth and guides to the path of the Mighty, the Praiseworthy.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those who have been given knowledge see that the Book you have brought is truth and verity. The verb “see” says here more than belief: it says a certitude that contemplates. While the deniers find in the proofs only matter to sink deeper into denial and obstinacy, those whom God has illumined discern in the Revelation the light that guides to the path of the Mighty, the Praiseworthy.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ع ل م
ع ز ز / ح م د

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا هَلْ نَدُلُّكُمْ عَلَىٰ رَجُلٍ يُنَبِّئُكُمْ إِذَا مُزِّقْتُمْ كُلَّ مُمَزَّقٍ إِنَّكُمْ لَفِي خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna kafarū hal nadullukum ʿalā rajulin yunabbiʾukum idhā muzziqtum kulla mumazzaqin innakum la-fī khalqin jadīdin
versets.trad: And those who disbelieve say: “Shall we point you to a man who tells you that, when you have been wholly torn to pieces, you will indeed be in a new creation?
versets.commentaire: The disbelievers pass derision back and forth: Qushayrī reports that they said to one another that they hold to be a lie what you announce — the scattering, the reckoning, and the resurrection. Turned to mockery, the promise of the “new creation” becomes in their eyes a madman's tale. Yet their sarcasm changes nothing of the decree: it increased in them only denial.

mots.sens: you are torn utterly to pieces

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ج د د

versets.ar: أَفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى اللَّهِ كَذِبًا أَم بِهِ جِنَّةٌ ۗ بَلِ الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ فِي الْعَذَابِ وَالضَّلَالِ الْبَعِيدِ
versets.tr: a-ftarā ʿalā llāhi kadhiban am bihi jinnatun bali lladhīna lā yuʾminūna bi-l-ākhirati fī l-ʿadhābi wa-ḍ-ḍalāli l-baʿīdi
versets.trad: Has he invented a lie against God, or is there madness in him?” Not at all; rather, those who do not believe in the Hereafter are in torment and far-reaching error.
versets.commentaire: Unable to refute the message, they waver between two slanders: a forged lie or madness. Qushayrī overturns the charge: it is not they who judge, but they who are judged. God set against them the proof of possibility through the very laws He has established in creation — yet this added only denial to them, and they met it only with stubbornness. Far from being the mark of madness, the refusal of the Hereafter is itself the torment and the most far-reaching error.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ب ع د

versets.ar: أَفَلَمْ يَرَوْا إِلَىٰ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُم مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۚ إِن نَّشَأْ نَخْسِفْ بِهِمُ الْأَرْضَ أَوْ نُسْقِطْ عَلَيْهِمْ كِسَفًا مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَةً لِّكُلِّ عَبْدٍ مُّنِيبٍ
versets.tr: a-fa-lam yaraw ilā mā bayna aydīhim wa-mā khalfahum mina s-samāʾi wa-l-arḍi in nashaʾ nakhsif bihimu l-arḍa aw nusqiṭ ʿalayhim kisafan mina s-samāʾi inna fī dhālika la-āyatan li-kulli ʿabdin munībin
versets.trad: Do they not see what is before them and what is behind them, of the sky and the earth? If We willed, We could cause the earth to swallow them, or cause a fragment of the sky to fall upon them. In that is surely a sign for every servant who turns to God.
versets.commentaire: The sky above, the earth below: man is hemmed in by signs that could at any moment turn into chastisement. Yet the verse closes not on the threat but on the opening: in that is a sign for every servant who turns back (munīb). Qushayrī will read throughout this sūra that the soul's turning back to God, through the confession of fault and the plea for pardon, is itself a grace; the sign speaks only to one whose heart is already turned toward return.

mots.def: khasafa: to make the earth collapse.

mots.racine: ع ب د / ن و ب
mots.def: munīb: who ceaselessly returns to God.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا دَاوُودَ مِنَّا فَضْلًا ۖ يَا جِبَالُ أَوِّبِي مَعَهُ وَالطَّيْرَ ۖ وَأَلَنَّا لَهُ الْحَدِيدَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ātaynā dāwūda minnā faḍlan yā jibālu awwibī maʿahu wa-ṭ-ṭayra wa-alannā lahu l-ḥadīda
versets.trad: And We gave David a special favor from Us: “O mountains, and you birds, echo back his songs of praise with him!” And We made iron soft for him,
versets.commentaire: On the nature of this “favor,” Qushayrī gathers several views: the mountains and birds answering his chant, which consoled him in his time of sorrow and weeping; or his return to God, after what befell him, through confession and plea; or that he contemplated his own indigence and that none but God could restore his state; or the sweetness of his voice in psalmody. Deeper still: “Not everything that cried out behind him had meaning: the meaning was with David, not with the mountains and birds.” And this sublime word: God revealed to him: “The moaning of sinners is dearer to Me than the clamor of worshippers.”

mots.def: to repeat the song of praise.

mots.racine: ل ي ن / ح د د
mots.sens: We made iron soft for him

versets.ar: أَنِ اعْمَلْ سَابِغَاتٍ وَقَدِّرْ فِي السَّرْدِ ۖ وَاعْمَلُوا صَالِحًا ۖ إِنِّي بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: ani-ʿmal sābighātin wa-qaddir fī s-sardi wa-ʿmalū ṣāliḥan innī bi-mā taʿmalūna baṣīrun
versets.trad: saying: “Make coats of mail and measure well the links. And work righteousness! I see clearly what you do.”
versets.commentaire: God made iron soft for David and made it a miracle, but also a livelihood. Qushayrī illumines the hidden wisdom: He placed therein the broadening of his provision, that he might find an honest labor in it, that he might cut off his desire to live at his community's expense — a lesson for whoever holds the station of imamate: let him take no profit from anyone, claim no wage, covet nothing from those who follow him. The “I see what you do” makes the very work an act beneath the gaze.

mots.racine: ق د ر / س ر د
mots.def: sard: the fitting of the rings.

versets.ar: وَلِسُلَيْمَانَ الرِّيحَ غُدُوُّهَا شَهْرٌ وَرَوَاحُهَا شَهْرٌ ۖ وَأَسَلْنَا لَهُ عَيْنَ الْقِطْرِ ۖ وَمِنَ الْجِنِّ مَن يَعْمَلُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِ ۖ وَمَن يَزِغْ مِنْهُمْ عَنْ أَمْرِنَا نُذِقْهُ مِنْ عَذَابِ السَّعِيرِ
versets.tr: wa-li-sulaymāna r-rīḥa ghuduwwuhā shahrun wa-rawāḥuhā shahrun wa-asalnā lahu ʿayna l-qiṭri wa-mina l-jinni man yaʿmalu bayna yadayhi bi-idhni rabbihi wa-man yazigh minhum ʿan amrinā nudhiqhu min ʿadhābi s-saʿīri
versets.trad: And to Solomon We subjected the wind: in the morning it journeyed a month's distance, and in the evening it returned a month's distance. And We made a spring of molten copper flow for him. And of the jinn, some worked before him by the leave of his Lord; and whoever of them deviated from Our command, We made him taste the torment of the Blaze.
versets.commentaire: The wind bore Solomon's carpet, a month's march in the morning, a month in the evening. Qushayrī relates the account in which Solomon, surveying his kingdom one day, felt the wind tilt his carpet; he commanded it: “Be straight!” And the wind answered: “Be straight yourself: so long as you are straight in your heart, I remain straight beneath you; when you tilted, I tilted.” Lesson: the stability of the subjected world depends on the inward uprightness of the one to whom it is subjected.

mots.racine: ع ي ن / ق ط ر

mots.def: zāgha: to swerve from the command.

versets.ar: يَعْمَلُونَ لَهُ مَا يَشَاءُ مِن مَّحَارِيبَ وَتَمَاثِيلَ وَجِفَانٍ كَالْجَوَابِ وَقُدُورٍ رَّاسِيَاتٍ ۚ اعْمَلُوا آلَ دَاوُودَ شُكْرًا ۚ وَقَلِيلٌ مِّنْ عِبَادِيَ الشَّكُورُ
versets.tr: yaʿmalūna lahu mā yashāʾu min maḥārība wa-tamāthīla wa-jifānin ka-l-jawābi wa-qudūrin rāsiyātin iʿmalū āla dāwūda shukran wa-qalīlun min ʿibādiya sh-shakūru
versets.trad: They made for him whatever he willed: sanctuaries, statues, basins as large as reservoirs, and cauldrons firmly set. Work, O family of David, in gratitude! And few of My servants are truly grateful.
versets.commentaire: Here the heart of the sūra: “Work in gratitude, O family of David.” Qushayrī meditates at length on shukr: the root of gratitude is increase. The shakūr is more than the grateful one: when men give thanks in ease, the shakūr gives thanks in trial; the grateful one thanks for the gift, the shakūr thanks even for the withholding. More subtly: few are those who receive grace from Me without ascribing it to secondary causes; most take the bounty of God, then ascribe its merit to other than Him, and thank other than Him. And David said: “My God, how shall I thank You, when I can do so only by means of a new grace from You?” And He revealed to him: “Once you have understood this, you have thanked Me.”

mots.racine: ج ف ن / ج ب ي

mots.def: intensive form: who gives thanks abundantly.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا قَضَيْنَا عَلَيْهِ الْمَوْتَ مَا دَلَّهُمْ عَلَىٰ مَوْتِهِ إِلَّا دَابَّةُ الْأَرْضِ تَأْكُلُ مِنسَأَتَهُ ۖ فَلَمَّا خَرَّ تَبَيَّنَتِ الْجِنُّ أَن لَّوْ كَانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ الْغَيْبَ مَا لَبِثُوا فِي الْعَذَابِ الْمُهِينِ
versets.tr: fa-lammā qaḍaynā ʿalayhi l-mawta mā dallahum ʿalā mawtihi illā dābbatu l-arḍi taʾkulu minsaʾatahu fa-lammā kharra tabayyanati l-jinnu an law kānū yaʿlamūna l-ghayba mā labithū fī l-ʿadhābi l-muhīni
versets.trad: Then when We decreed his death, nothing pointed them to his death but a creature of the earth that gnawed away his staff. And when he fell, it became clear to the jinn that, had they known the unseen, they would not have remained in the humiliating torment.
themes: the unseen veiled from the jinn
versets.commentaire: Solomon died leaning on his staff, and none knew it: the jinn went on working, believing him alive, until the worm gnawed the staff and the body fell. Qushayrī draws the political and spiritual lesson: such is the kingdom that stands only by another, whose support is but a staff: when the support falls, the kingdom falls with it; whoever subsists by another vanishes the moment that other vanishes. Only what leans upon God endures.

mots.def: the scepter on which he leaned.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ه و ن

versets.ar: لَقَدْ كَانَ لِسَبَإٍ فِي مَسْكَنِهِمْ آيَةٌ ۖ جَنَّتَانِ عَن يَمِينٍ وَشِمَالٍ ۖ كُلُوا مِن رِّزْقِ رَبِّكُمْ وَاشْكُرُوا لَهُ ۚ بَلْدَةٌ طَيِّبَةٌ وَرَبٌّ غَفُورٌ
versets.tr: laqad kāna li-sabaʾin fī maskanihim āyatun jannatāni ʿan yamīnin wa-shimālin kulū min rizqi rabbikum wa-shkurū lahu baldatun ṭayyibatun wa-rabbun ghafūrun
versets.trad: There was for Sheba, in their dwelling place, a sign: two gardens, one on the right and one on the left. “Eat of the provision of your Lord and be grateful to Him: a good land and a forgiving Lord!”
versets.commentaire: The people of Sheba lived in abundance, security, and ease. Qushayrī: they were commanded to be patient in well-being and to give thanks for grace — an easy and light matter; but they turned away from accord, denied the bounty, and neglected gratitude. So their state was changed, as it was said: “You changed and we changed — O sorrow for one who seeks a substitute for Salmā and finds none.”

mots.def: orchards to the right and left.

mots.racine: ب ل د / ط ي ب

versets.ar: فَأَعْرَضُوا فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ سَيْلَ الْعَرِمِ وَبَدَّلْنَاهُم بِجَنَّتَيْهِمْ جَنَّتَيْنِ ذَوَاتَيْ أُكُلٍ خَمْطٍ وَأَثْلٍ وَشَيْءٍ مِّن سِدْرٍ قَلِيلٍ
versets.tr: fa-aʿraḍū fa-arsalnā ʿalayhim sayla l-ʿarimi wa-baddalnāhum bi-jannatayhim jannatayni dhawātay ukulin khamṭin wa-athlin wa-shayʾin min sidrin qalīlin
versets.trad: But they turned away, so We loosed upon them the flood of the dam, and We exchanged their two gardens for two gardens bearing bitter fruit, tamarisks, and a few lote trees.
versets.commentaire: Ingratitude called forth the flood of the dam (sayl al-ʿarim): the gardens of sweetness became bitter thickets. Qushayrī draws from it a warning for the wayfarer: so it is with one who lives in the ease of state, the chaining of grace, the joy of the heart, and the favor of the moment: let him commit a slip, fail in courtesy, or follow a desire without knowing the worth of what he possesses, and the state turns over — no moment, no state, no joy, no union; the day darkens upon him though his nights were luminous.

mots.racine: س ي ل / ع ر م
mots.def: the breaking of the Maʾrib dam.

mots.racine: أ ك ل / خ م ط
أ ث ل / س د ر

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ جَزَيْنَاهُم بِمَا كَفَرُوا ۖ وَهَلْ نُجَازِي إِلَّا الْكَفُورَ
versets.tr: dhālika jazaynāhum bi-mā kafarū wa-hal nujāzī illā l-kafūra
versets.trad: Thus We requited them for their ingratitude. Do We requite thus any but the ingrate?
versets.commentaire: The punishment was but the exact measure of their deed. Qushayrī hammers it home in images: they were treated only according to what they deserved, drank only of what they had corrupted, fell only into the pit they had dug, and were slain only by the sword they had forged. The sentence “Do We requite any but the ingrate (al-kafūr)” opposes term for term the shakūr of verse 13: the surpassing ingrate receives the punishment, as the surpassing grateful one receives the grace.

mots.def: to repay according to the deed.

versets.ar: وَجَعَلْنَا بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ الْقُرَى الَّتِي بَارَكْنَا فِيهَا قُرًى ظَاهِرَةً وَقَدَّرْنَا فِيهَا السَّيْرَ ۖ سِيرُوا فِيهَا لَيَالِيَ وَأَيَّامًا آمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-jaʿalnā baynahum wa-bayna l-qurā llatī bāraknā fīhā quran ẓāhiratan wa-qaddarnā fīhā s-sayra sīrū fīhā layāliya wa-ayyāman āminīna
versets.trad: And between them and the towns We had blessed We placed prominent towns as waystations, and We measured the journey between them: “Travel through them by night and by day in safety.”
versets.commentaire: God had marked their road with prosperous, safe towns, journeys measured by day and night. Everything in that land invited them to gratitude. Qushayrī sums up their response: their only conduct was persistence in disobedience and obstinacy in their straying and excess. The offered safety served only to deepen their ingratitude when they disdained it.

mots.racine: ق ر ي / ظ ه ر

versets.ar: فَقَالُوا رَبَّنَا بَاعِدْ بَيْنَ أَسْفَارِنَا وَظَلَمُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ فَجَعَلْنَاهُمْ أَحَادِيثَ وَمَزَّقْنَاهُمْ كُلَّ مُمَزَّقٍ ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِّكُلِّ صَبَّارٍ شَكُورٍ
versets.tr: fa-qālū rabbanā bāʿid bayna asfārinā wa-ẓalamū anfusahum fa-jaʿalnāhum aḥādītha wa-mazzaqnāhum kulla mumazzaqin inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-kulli ṣabbārin shakūrin
versets.trad: But they said: “Our Lord, lengthen the distances between our journeys!” They wronged themselves; so We made them tales and tore them utterly to pieces. In that are surely signs for every patient, grateful one.
themes: signs for the patient and grateful
versets.commentaire: Weary of ease, they themselves demanded that the stages be drawn apart — a senseless wish that turned into dispersal. Qushayrī: We scattered them so utterly that they became a proverb: “they went off like the hands of Sheba,” “they dispersed like the tribes of Sheba.” And the verse seals on the saint's twofold title: in their story are signs for every being patient in trial, grateful in grace (ṣabbār shakūr).

mots.racine: ص ب ر / ش ك ر
mots.def: two intensive forms of the saint.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ صَدَّقَ عَلَيْهِمْ إِبْلِيسُ ظَنَّهُ فَاتَّبَعُوهُ إِلَّا فَرِيقًا مِّنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ṣaddaqa ʿalayhim iblīsu ẓannahu fa-ttabaʿūhu illā farīqan mina l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: And Iblīs proved his conjecture about them true, so they followed him, except for a group of believers.
versets.commentaire: Iblīs saw his conjecture about them verified. But Qushayrī at once strips Satan of all real power: Iblīs has nothing at his own disposal; over his followers, jinn and men, he has only a substanceless ascendancy, and of misguidance he possesses nothing. For if he could harm another, he could keep himself upon guidance — according to the word: “Over My servants you have no power.” The “group of believers” escapes because it leans not on itself but on God.

mots.def: to make his supposition come true.

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ لَهُ عَلَيْهِم مِّن سُلْطَانٍ إِلَّا لِنَعْلَمَ مَن يُؤْمِنُ بِالْآخِرَةِ مِمَّنْ هُوَ مِنْهَا فِي شَكٍّ ۗ وَرَبُّكَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ حَفِيظٌ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna lahu ʿalayhim min sulṭānin illā li-naʿlama man yuʾminu bi-l-ākhirati mimman huwa minhā fī shakkin wa-rabbuka ʿalā kulli shayʾin ḥafīẓun
versets.trad: He had no power over them, except that We might distinguish him who believes in the Hereafter from him who is in doubt of it. And your Lord is Guardian over all things.
versets.commentaire: The verse confirms: Satan has no power, save to serve as a trial that distinguishes the believer from the doubter. Then Qushayrī rises toward majesty: God makes known that He is alone in His kingship, unique in His divinity, glorious above rivals and equals; that those have not an atom's weight, are neither helper nor partner nor support, neither here below nor in the Hereafter; and that the angels in heaven are seized with awe, standing where the Truth has fixed them, never wearying of His worship nor rebelling.

mots.def: who watches over and preserves all.

versets.ar: قُلِ ادْعُوا الَّذِينَ زَعَمْتُم مِّن دُونِ اللَّهِ ۖ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَلَا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَمَا لَهُمْ فِيهِمَا مِن شِرْكٍ وَمَا لَهُ مِنْهُم مِّن ظَهِيرٍ
versets.tr: quli-dʿū lladhīna zaʿamtum min dūni llāhi lā yamlikūna mithqāla dharratin fī s-samāwāti wa-lā fī l-arḍi wa-mā lahum fīhimā min shirkin wa-mā lahu minhum min ẓahīrin
versets.trad: Say: “Call upon those you claim to be gods besides God! They possess not an atom's weight, neither in the heavens nor on the earth; they have no share in either, and He has from them no helper.”
versets.commentaire: A challenge flung at the false gods: let them be invoked, they possess nothing. Qushayrī sets this verse within the great proclamation of oneness: the alleged partners possess not even an atom's weight nor a grain's measure; they are of no help, no share, no support. The nothingness of the idols is the negative mirror of the divine fullness — there is no King but God; none shares His kingship.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَنفَعُ الشَّفَاعَةُ عِندَهُ إِلَّا لِمَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ ۚ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا فُزِّعَ عَن قُلُوبِهِمْ قَالُوا مَاذَا قَالَ رَبُّكُمْ ۖ قَالُوا الْحَقَّ ۖ وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُّ الْكَبِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-lā tanfaʿu sh-shafāʿatu ʿindahu illā li-man adhina lahu ḥattā idhā fuzziʿa ʿan qulūbihim qālū mādhā qāla rabbukum qālū l-ḥaqqa wa-huwa l-ʿaliyyu l-kabīru
versets.trad: No intercession avails with Him except for one whom He permits. When terror is lifted from their hearts, they will be asked: “What did your Lord say?” They will answer: “The Truth! And He is the Most High, the Most Great!”
versets.commentaire: Intercession avails only by divine permission: none has any hold on grace. When terror is lifted from hearts, the only possible answer to “What did your Lord say?” is: “The Truth.” The whole movement of the sūra converges here — set against the powerlessness of the idols and the presumed intercessors, God remains alone the Most High, the Most Great, glorious above rivals and equals.

mots.sens: terror is lifted from their hearts

mots.racine: ع ل و / ك ب ر
mots.sens: the Most High, the Most Great

versets.ar: قُلْ مَن يَرْزُقُكُم مِّنَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۖ قُلِ اللَّهُ ۖ وَإِنَّا أَوْ إِيَّاكُمْ لَعَلَىٰ هُدًى أَوْ فِي ضَلَالٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: qul man yarzuqukum mina s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi quli llāhu wa-innā aw iyyākum la-ʿalā hudan aw fī ḍalālin mubīnin
versets.trad: Say: “Who provides for you from the heavens and the earth?” Say: “God! And surely either we or you are upon guidance or in manifest error.”
versets.commentaire: An irresistible argument: they themselves ascribe provision to none but God. Qushayrī: none, despite his idolatry, has ever claimed that provision depends on any but God; and just as He has no partner in provision nor in creation, He has none in the right to worship and glorification. The phrasing “either we or you upon guidance or in error” is a courtesy of dispute that lets the adversary draw the conclusion himself.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ب ي ن

versets.ar: قُل لَّا تُسْأَلُونَ عَمَّا أَجْرَمْنَا وَلَا نُسْأَلُ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: qul lā tusʾalūna ʿammā ajramnā wa-lā nusʾalu ʿammā taʿmalūna
versets.trad: Say: “You will not be questioned about what we have done wrong, nor shall we be questioned about what you do.”
versets.commentaire: Each answers only for himself. Qushayrī: on the Day of gathering, God calls each to account for his own deeds, holds none responsible for another's deed; each receives his own book, and God demands of each soul its own reckoning. The verse cuts short all solidarity in fault: none will be questioned about another's crime.

versets.ar: قُلْ يَجْمَعُ بَيْنَنَا رَبُّنَا ثُمَّ يَفْتَحُ بَيْنَنَا بِالْحَقِّ وَهُوَ الْفَتَّاحُ الْعَلِيمُ
versets.tr: qul yajmaʿu baynanā rabbunā thumma yaftaḥu baynanā bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-huwa l-fattāḥu l-ʿalīmu
versets.trad: Say: “Our Lord will gather us together, then He will judge between us with truth, for He is the Decider, the All-Knowing.”
versets.commentaire: God will gather, then judge with truth. Qushayrī dwells on the hidden grace of the “gathering”: God has made it His way to gather His servants, then to treat them in assembly otherwise than He treats them in dispersion: the gathering has an immense effect in the Law, and prayer in congregation a particular efficacy. He blames those who separated from the Prophet and praises whoever left him only with leave: the masters await from the assembly increases of grace and find their solace in this verse.

mots.racine: ف ت ح / ع ل م
mots.def: who judges supremely and knows all.

versets.ar: قُلْ أَرُونِيَ الَّذِينَ أَلْحَقْتُم بِهِ شُرَكَاءَ ۖ كَلَّا ۚ بَلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: qul arūniya lladhīna alḥaqtum bihi shurakāʾa kallā bal huwa llāhu l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: Say: “Show me those you have attached to Him as partners.” Not at all! Rather, He alone is God, the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: “Show me!”: a summons nothing can satisfy. Qushayrī dismantles the spring of idolatry: after assuring themselves that these are inert objects that neither understand nor are able, that neither hear nor see, the suspicion still came to them that they merited worship; and when summoned to furnish the proof, they invoked nothing but the imitation of their forefathers — and this is the most far-reaching error and the most manifest loss.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م

versets.ar: وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَاكَ إِلَّا كَافَّةً لِّلنَّاسِ بَشِيرًا وَنَذِيرًا وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā arsalnāka illā kāffatan li-n-nāsi bashīran wa-nadhīran wa-lākinna akthara n-nāsi lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: We have sent you only to all mankind, as a bearer of glad tidings and a warner; but most of mankind do not know.
versets.commentaire: The Prophet is sent to the whole of mankind. Qushayrī unfolds the grandeur of this sending: We have sent you, upheld by miracles, honored with all attributes, lord in the lands and the heavens, manifest to the people of faith, veiled from the sight of the people of denial — though to their eyes you are visible to the gaze, according to the word: “You see them looking at you, but they do not see.” The blindness is not in the message but in the eye that beholds it.

versets.ar: وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَٰذَا الْوَعْدُ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yaqūlūna matā hādhā l-waʿdu in kuntum ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: And they say: “When is this promise, if you are truthful?”
versets.commentaire: They mock by demanding the date of the promise. Qushayrī: so often do they repeat this word that God has repeated it in His Book, reported from them; and the answer is: you have an appointed term. The question is no quest for truth but an evasion: to demand the deadline in order to defer faith.

versets.ar: قُل لَّكُم مِّيعَادُ يَوْمٍ لَّا تَسْتَأْخِرُونَ عَنْهُ سَاعَةً وَلَا تَسْتَقْدِمُونَ
versets.tr: qul lakum mīʿādu yawmin lā tastaʾkhirūna ʿanhu sāʿatan wa-lā tastaqdimūna
versets.trad: Say: “You have an appointed Day which you can neither delay by an hour nor advance.”
versets.commentaire: To their challenge, the answer is calm and firm. Qushayrī: you have the appointment of a Day, and at that appointment you can neither delay by an hour nor advance. The term is sealed in the decree: neither haste nor procrastination will move it. Knowing the date matters less than the certitude of its coming.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَن نُّؤْمِنَ بِهَٰذَا الْقُرْآنِ وَلَا بِالَّذِي بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ۗ وَلَوْ تَرَىٰ إِذِ الظَّالِمُونَ مَوْقُوفُونَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ يَرْجِعُ بَعْضُهُمْ إِلَىٰ بَعْضٍ الْقَوْلَ يَقُولُ الَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا لِلَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا لَوْلَا أَنتُمْ لَكُنَّا مُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna kafarū lan nuʾmina bi-hādhā l-qurʾāni wa-lā bi-lladhī bayna yadayhi wa-law tarā idhi ẓ-ẓālimūna mawqūfūna ʿinda rabbihim yarjiʿu baʿḍuhum ilā baʿḍin il-qawla yaqūlu lladhīna stuḍʿifū li-lladhīna stakbarū lawlā antum la-kunnā muʾminīna
versets.trad: Those who disbelieve say: “We will never believe in this Qurʾān, nor in what came before it.” If only you could see when the wrongdoers are made to stand before their Lord, hurling words back at one another. Those who were oppressed will say to those who were arrogant: “Had it not been for you, we would have been believers.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the dreadful scene: if you saw them on that day, you would see a terrible sight: they hurl words back at one another, each casting the fault upon the other. The weak accuse the arrogant of having led them astray, the arrogant deny it. And he extends the image to the very limbs: likewise, tomorrow, the organs will testify against one another: the hand will say “you took,” the eye “I looked”; and the very discord is a chastisement. Whoever lives in disobedience, God sets against him all that was most submissive to him.

versets.ar: قَالَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا لِلَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا أَنَحْنُ صَدَدْنَاكُمْ عَنِ الْهُدَىٰ بَعْدَ إِذْ جَاءَكُم ۖ بَلْ كُنتُم مُّجْرِمِينَ
versets.tr: qāla lladhīna stakbarū li-lladhīna stuḍʿifū a-naḥnu ṣadadnākum ʿani l-hudā baʿda idh jāʾakum bal kuntum mujrimīna
versets.trad: Those who were arrogant will say to those who were oppressed: “Was it we who turned you away from guidance after it came to you? No! Rather, you yourselves were criminals.”
versets.commentaire: The arrogant turn the accusation around: it was not we, it was you yourselves. Qushayrī: the arrogant deny and say: “Rather it was you who followed us”; such are the companions of fault, friends in perdition. According to the word: “That Day, friends will be enemies to one another.” Complicity in evil unravels into mutual accusations the moment the veil falls.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا لِلَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا بَلْ مَكْرُ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ إِذْ تَأْمُرُونَنَا أَن نَّكْفُرَ بِاللَّهِ وَنَجْعَلَ لَهُ أَندَادًا ۚ وَأَسَرُّوا النَّدَامَةَ لَمَّا رَأَوُا الْعَذَابَ وَجَعَلْنَا الْأَغْلَالَ فِي أَعْنَاقِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ۚ هَلْ يُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna stuḍʿifū li-lladhīna stakbarū bal makru l-layli wa-n-nahāri idh taʾmurūnanā an nakfura bi-llāhi wa-najʿala lahu andādan wa-asarrū n-nadāmata lammā raʾawu l-ʿadhāba wa-jaʿalnā l-aghlāla fī aʿnāqi lladhīna kafarū hal yujzawna illā mā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: And those who were oppressed will say to those who were arrogant: “Rather, it was your scheming by night and day, when you commanded us to disbelieve in God and to set up rivals to Him.” And they will conceal their remorse when they see the punishment; and We will place shackles on the necks of those who disbelieved. Are they requited for anything but what they used to do?
versets.commentaire: The weak reply: it was your ceaseless plotting, night and day, that drove us to disbelieve. And all conceal their remorse when they see the punishment. Qushayrī: if only they had known, they would have meditated; had they meditated, they would have believed and been guided — but let the decree that was to be accomplished be accomplished. Regret comes too late: the requital is but the exact mirror of the deed.

mots.sens: the scheming by night and day

mots.racine: س ر ر / ن د م

versets.ar: وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا فِي قَرْيَةٍ مِّن نَّذِيرٍ إِلَّا قَالَ مُتْرَفُوهَا إِنَّا بِمَا أُرْسِلْتُم بِهِ كَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā arsalnā fī qaryatin min nadhīrin illā qāla mutrafūhā innā bi-mā ursiltum bihi kāfirūna
versets.trad: We never sent a warner to a town but its affluent ones declared: “We disbelieve in the message you have been sent with,”
versets.commentaire: Always it is those sated with wealth who rise first against the messenger. Qushayrī: they met Our messengers with denial, and Our messengers were patient. Yet what would it have cost these disbelievers to believe? It was for their salvation that they were sent, for their good that they were called and warned; had they accorded with them, they would have been happy — but portions were already decreed, verdicts already rendered, and God prevails over His affair.

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا نَحْنُ أَكْثَرُ أَمْوَالًا وَأَوْلَادًا وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū naḥnu aktharu amwālan wa-awlādan wa-mā naḥnu bi-muʿadhdhabīna
versets.trad: and they said: “We have more wealth and children, and we shall not be punished.”
versets.commentaire: They think their abundance a safeguard against chastisement. Qushayrī cuts through the illusion: it is not a matter of the abundance of wealth and children; it is a matter of inner sight, opened for one people and closed for another. Riches are no sign of election: the true division is played out in the light given or withheld from the heart, not in the measure of holdings.

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّي يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَاءُ وَيَقْدِرُ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: qul inna rabbī yabsuṭu r-rizqa li-man yashāʾu wa-yaqdiru wa-lākinna akthara n-nāsi lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: Say: “In truth, my Lord extends provision to whom He wills and restricts it, but most of mankind do not know.”
versets.commentaire: The apportioning of provision — bounty here, measure there — belongs to a wisdom most do not know. Abundance is no favor, nor restriction disgrace: it is a decree whose meaning escapes one who sees only the outward. Qushayrī recalls elsewhere that the true division is played out in the inner sight, opened or closed, not in the quantity distributed.

mots.def: qadara: to reduce, fix the measure.

versets.ar: وَمَا أَمْوَالُكُمْ وَلَا أَوْلَادُكُم بِالَّتِي تُقَرِّبُكُمْ عِندَنَا زُلْفَىٰ إِلَّا مَنْ آمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا فَأُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمْ جَزَاءُ الضِّعْفِ بِمَا عَمِلُوا وَهُمْ فِي الْغُرُفَاتِ آمِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā amwālukum wa-lā awlādukum bi-llatī tuqarribukum ʿindanā zulfā illā man āmana wa-ʿamila ṣāliḥan fa-ulāʾika lahum jazāʾu ḍ-ḍiʿfi bi-mā ʿamilū wa-hum fī l-ghurufāti āminūna
versets.trad: Neither your wealth nor your children are what bring you near to Us in rank, except for those who believe and do righteousness: for them is a double reward for what they did, and they will be secure in the lofty dwellings.
versets.commentaire: Neither gold nor offspring draw near to God. Qushayrī: nearness is not gained by wealth or children, but by pure works, limpid states, purified breaths; nay, by the prior solicitude, the guidance that follows, and the truthful care. The “double reward” is multiplied beyond that of earlier peoples; and “secure in the lofty dwellings” means for Qushayrī: safe from the troubling of purity and from expulsion from the Garden.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / ض ع ف

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يَسْعَوْنَ فِي آيَاتِنَا مُعَاجِزِينَ أُولَٰئِكَ فِي الْعَذَابِ مُحْضَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yasʿawna fī āyātinā muʿājizīna ulāʾika fī l-ʿadhābi muḥḍarūna
versets.trad: And those who strive against Our Signs, seeking to thwart them — those will be brought into the torment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives here an unexpected inward reading: these are those who do not honor the saints, who do not observe the right of God in secret; they are in the torment of their opposition to the saints of God, in the torment of falling, through the ill omen of that, into the things God has forbidden, then in the torment of falling from the gaze of God. Three nested torments: the disrespect, the transgression that follows, and the loss of the divine vision.

mots.def: to seek to defy the Signs.
dragged by force to the torment.

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّي يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ وَيَقْدِرُ لَهُ ۚ وَمَا أَنفَقْتُم مِّن شَيْءٍ فَهُوَ يُخْلِفُهُ ۖ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ
versets.tr: qul inna rabbī yabsuṭu r-rizqa li-man yashāʾu min ʿibādihi wa-yaqdiru lahu wa-mā anfaqtum min shayʾin fa-huwa yukhlifuhu wa-huwa khayru r-rāziqīna
versets.trad: Say: “In truth, my Lord extends provision to whom He wills of His servants and restricts it for him. And whatever you spend, He will replace it, for He is the best of providers.”
versets.commentaire: What is spent for God, He replaces. Qushayrī raises “replacement” far beyond the material: among the substitutes of this world is contentment in privation and loss — more perfect than the joy of possessed goods; and there is intimacy with God in seclusion, which is gained only in stripping away. The true “return” is not a return of goods, but the gift of nearness.

mots.racine: خ ي ر / ر ز ق

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ يَقُولُ لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ أَهَٰؤُلَاءِ إِيَّاكُمْ كَانُوا يَعْبُدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma yaḥshuruhum jamīʿan thumma yaqūlu li-l-malāʾikati a-hāʾulāʾi iyyākum kānū yaʿbudūna
versets.trad: And on the Day He gathers them all together, then says to the angels: “Was it you these people used to worship?”
versets.commentaire: On the Day of gathering, God questions the angels about those who worshipped them. Qushayrī: a people used to worship the angels; He puts them to the test concerning them, and the angels disown them, magnify God and glorify Him — and those people are put to shame. And he adds, terribly: the shame at the interrogation is one of the harshest of chastisements; tomorrow some will say: “Punish us, Lord, with whatever torments You will, but do not punish us with this question!”

versets.ar: قَالُوا سُبْحَانَكَ أَنتَ وَلِيُّنَا مِن دُونِهِم ۖ بَلْ كَانُوا يَعْبُدُونَ الْجِنَّ ۖ أَكْثَرُهُم بِهِم مُّؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: qālū subḥānaka anta waliyyunā min dūnihim bal kānū yaʿbudūna l-jinna aktharuhum bihim muʾminūna
versets.trad: They will say: “Glory be to You! You are our Protector, not they. Rather, they used to worship the jinn; most of them believed in them.”
versets.commentaire: The angels disown them: “Glory be to You, You are our Protector, not they.” They unveil that the real object of worship was the jinn whispering misguidance. Qushayrī: the angels clear themselves, magnify God and glorify Him — and the guilty are then put to shame. The verse severs every bond of patronage between the worshipped and their worshippers: no intercessor acknowledges those who have wrongly deified him.

versets.ar: فَالْيَوْمَ لَا يَمْلِكُ بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ نَّفْعًا وَلَا ضَرًّا وَنَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ذُوقُوا عَذَابَ النَّارِ الَّتِي كُنتُم بِهَا تُكَذِّبُونَ
versets.tr: fa-l-yawma lā yamliku baʿḍukum li-baʿḍin nafʿan wa-lā ḍarran wa-naqūlu li-lladhīna ẓalamū dhūqū ʿadhāba n-nāri llatī kuntum bihā tukadhdhibūna
versets.trad: So today none of you has power to benefit or harm another, and We say to those who did wrong: “Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny!”
versets.commentaire: That Day, none benefits or harms another. Qushayrī draws a piercing allusion: whoever has hung his heart upon creatures, believing the mending of his state to lie in scheming and leaning on his fellows, God tears mercy from his heart and abandons him; He confounds his states: neither do his fellows avail him at all, nor does his reason give him any clear sight, nor has he any return to God; and if he returns, He shows him no mercy. To lean on other than God is to doom oneself to abandonment.

mots.def: no power to help or harm.

mots.racine: ن ف ع / ض ر ر

versets.ar: وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُنَا بَيِّنَاتٍ قَالُوا مَا هَٰذَا إِلَّا رَجُلٌ يُرِيدُ أَن يَصُدَّكُمْ عَمَّا كَانَ يَعْبُدُ آبَاؤُكُمْ وَقَالُوا مَا هَٰذَا إِلَّا إِفْكٌ مُّفْتَرًى ۚ وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَاءَهُمْ إِنْ هَٰذَا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-idhā tutlā ʿalayhim āyātunā bayyinātin qālū mā hādhā illā rajulun yurīdu an yaṣuddakum ʿammā kāna yaʿbudu ābāʾukum wa-qālū mā hādhā illā ifkun muftaran wa-qāla lladhīna kafarū li-l-ḥaqqi lammā jāʾahum in hādhā illā siḥrun mubīnun
versets.trad: And when Our clear verses are recited to them, they say: “This is but a man who wishes to turn you away from what your fathers worshipped,” and they say: “This is but an invented falsehood.” And those who disbelieve say of the Truth when it reaches them: “This is nothing but manifest magic!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads here the war waged against the spiritual guides: the sages and the saints — who are the imams on this path — when they direct people toward God, behold, an evil friend, like a false counsellor among the heedless and the sons of the world, says to the disciple: “What is this? Who can endure all this? You may not complete the path! You must have the world while you live!” — until this wretch falters in accepting the counsel, and so perishes and goes astray.

mots.racine: أ ف ك / ف ر ي
س ح ر / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَمَا آتَيْنَاهُم مِّن كُتُبٍ يَدْرُسُونَهَا ۖ وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا إِلَيْهِمْ قَبْلَكَ مِن نَّذِيرٍ
versets.tr: wa-mā ātaynāhum min kutubin yadrusūnahā wa-mā arsalnā ilayhim qablaka min nadhīrin
versets.trad: We had given them no scriptures to study, nor sent them any warner before you.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī applies the verse to the opposition of the heedless against the people of the heart: the heedless contest the men of the heart over what comes to pass, through the trouble their soul breathes into them and the vain thoughts that cross their minds — without support from an inspiration or from a decree understood of God. Now the people of the realities, who are the tongue of the moment, when they say or utter something, cannot produce its proof, for one who speaks by spiritual insight or inspiration cannot buttress his words. So the way of these great ones is to be silent — then the days will answer for them.

versets.ar: وَكَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ وَمَا بَلَغُوا مِعْشَارَ مَا آتَيْنَاهُمْ فَكَذَّبُوا رُسُلِي ۖ فَكَيْفَ كَانَ نَكِيرِ
versets.tr: wa-kadhdhaba lladhīna min qablihim wa-mā balaghū miʿshāra mā ātaynāhum fa-kadhdhabū rusulī fa-kayfa kāna nakīri
versets.trad: Those before them also denied, and these have not attained a tenth of what We gave them; yet they denied My messengers. So how was My reproach!
versets.commentaire: The peoples of old, far mightier, also denied the messengers — and terrible was the reproach. The verse sets the Meccans within a long chain of punished ingratitude: their present strength is nothing beside that which was destroyed. “How was My reproach” is a question that calls for meditation upon the ruins, an echo of the people of Sheba whose story is “a sign for every patient, grateful one.”

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنَّمَا أَعِظُكُم بِوَاحِدَةٍ ۖ أَن تَقُومُوا لِلَّهِ مَثْنَىٰ وَفُرَادَىٰ ثُمَّ تَتَفَكَّرُوا ۚ مَا بِصَاحِبِكُم مِّن جِنَّةٍ ۚ إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ لَّكُم بَيْنَ يَدَيْ عَذَابٍ شَدِيدٍ
versets.tr: qul innamā aʿiẓukum bi-wāḥidatin an taqūmū li-llāhi mathnā wa-furādā thumma tatafakkarū mā bi-ṣāḥibikum min jinnatin in huwa illā nadhīrun lakum bayna yaday ʿadhābin shadīdin
versets.trad: Say: “I exhort you to one thing only: that you stand before God, in pairs and singly, then reflect. There is no madness in your companion; he is only a warner to you before a severe punishment.”
versets.commentaire: A single exhortation: to stand before God and reflect. Qushayrī makes the verse speak like an examination: when your soul tempts you to deny the Messenger, look closely: do you see in him the marks of what you accuse him of? Here is Muḥammad: you say he is a magician — where are the traces of magic on his states and deeds? You say a poet — to which kind of poetry does his speech belong? You say possessed — what madness has appeared from him? And since you are incapable of this, why do you not acknowledge that he is truthful?

mots.racine: ث ن ي / ف ر د

versets.ar: قُلْ مَا سَأَلْتُكُم مِّنْ أَجْرٍ فَهُوَ لَكُمْ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِيَ إِلَّا عَلَى اللَّهِ ۖ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيدٌ
versets.tr: qul mā saʾaltukum min ajrin fa-huwa lakum in ajriya illā ʿalā llāhi wa-huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin shahīdun
versets.trad: Say: “Whatever wage I have asked of you, it is yours; my wage rests only upon God, and He is Witness over all things.”
versets.commentaire: The Messenger claims no wage: all his recompense rests only upon God. This rejoins the teaching given concerning David (v. 11): let whoever holds the station of imamate take no profit from anyone, claim no wage, covet nothing from those who follow him. Total selflessness is the seal of authenticity; and “He is Witness over all things” founds this disinterest on the gaze of God alone.

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنَّ رَبِّي يَقْذِفُ بِالْحَقِّ عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ
versets.tr: qul inna rabbī yaqdhifu bi-l-ḥaqqi ʿallāmu l-ghuyūbi
versets.trad: Say: “Truly, my Lord casts the Truth — the Knower of all that is hidden.”
versets.commentaire: “My Lord casts the Truth”: a verse Qushayrī unfolds in three victorious casts. He casts the Truth upon the falsehood of the heedless: their stratagems collapse and their impotence breaks forth. He casts the Truth upon the states of the opposers: their boldness dissolves and the ill omen of their sins envelops them. He casts the Truth — when the people of spiritual meanings appear — upon the darkness of the people of pretension: He quenches their tumult, exposes them on the spot, and lays bare their flaw.

mots.def: the true that crushes the false.

mots.racine: ع ل م / غ ي ب

versets.ar: قُلْ جَاءَ الْحَقُّ وَمَا يُبْدِئُ الْبَاطِلُ وَمَا يُعِيدُ
versets.tr: qul jāʾa l-ḥaqqu wa-mā yubdiʾu l-bāṭilu wa-mā yuʿīdu
versets.trad: Say: “The Truth has come; falsehood can neither originate anything nor restore it.”
versets.commentaire: The true has come; the false can produce nothing nor bring anything back. Qushayrī makes of it a law of history: falsehood, with the passing of days, only vanishes the more, and the Truth, with the passing of days, only grows in strength and splendor. Time does not wear out the true: it reveals it; it does not establish the false: it dissolves it.

mots.racine: ج ي أ / ح ق ق

versets.ar: قُلْ إِن ضَلَلْتُ فَإِنَّمَا أَضِلُّ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِي ۖ وَإِنِ اهْتَدَيْتُ فَبِمَا يُوحِي إِلَيَّ رَبِّي ۚ إِنَّهُ سَمِيعٌ قَرِيبٌ
versets.tr: qul in ḍalaltu fa-innamā aḍillu ʿalā nafsī wa-ini htadaytu fa-bimā yūḥī ilayya rabbī innahu samīʿun qarībun
versets.trad: Say: “If I have gone astray, I go astray only against myself. And if I am guided, it is by what my Lord reveals to me. Truly, He is All-Hearing, Near.”
versets.commentaire: All guidance comes from God, all straying falls back upon the soul. Qushayrī has the Prophet speak to the deniers: if I am guided, it is by my Lord, not by my effort; and if in your eyes I am astray, the harm of my straying falls only upon me and will not hurt you. Look then to yourselves: where have you fallen? What harm would you suffer by obeying me? You would lose nothing in the moment, weary yourselves in nothing, lose nothing of your rank. And the verse seals on gentleness: He hears, He is near.

mots.racine: س م ع / ق ر ب

versets.ar: وَلَوْ تَرَىٰ إِذْ فَزِعُوا فَلَا فَوْتَ وَأُخِذُوا مِن مَّكَانٍ قَرِيبٍ
versets.tr: wa-law tarā idh faziʿū fa-lā fawta wa-ukhidhū min makānin qarībin
versets.trad: If only you could see when they are terrified, with no escape, and are seized from a place near at hand.
versets.commentaire: “If you saw their terror!” Qushayrī: if you saw that, you would see a fearful sight, an immense affair: when He seizes them after the respite, there is nothing but extermination. The “near place” says that no refuge sets one far from the divine grasp: the chastisement springs up at the very spot where one thought oneself safe. The dread that seizes them is that of one who deferred until all flight was closed.

mots.def: faziʿa: to be seized with dread.

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا آمَنَّا بِهِ وَأَنَّىٰ لَهُمُ التَّنَاوُشُ مِن مَّكَانٍ بَعِيدٍ
versets.tr: wa-qālū āmannā bihi wa-annā lahumu t-tanāwushu min makānin baʿīdin
versets.trad: And they will say: “We believe in Him!” But how can they attain it from a place so far away,
versets.commentaire: At the hour of chastisement, they cry their faith — but how to attain it from a place so distant? Qushayrī: they repent when the doors have closed, they regret when the bonds have snapped: there remain then only sighs and remorse — and it is no longer the hour of repentance! Such too is the lot of one who scorns the details of his torpor and does not rouse from his heedlessness: there comes to him at last a rejection from the Truth, and a veil receives him.

versets.ar: وَقَدْ كَفَرُوا بِهِ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَيَقْذِفُونَ بِالْغَيْبِ مِن مَّكَانٍ بَعِيدٍ
versets.tr: wa-qad kafarū bihi min qablu wa-yaqdhifūna bi-l-ghaybi min makānin baʿīdin
versets.trad: while they had disbelieved in it before, and hurled conjectures at the unseen from a place far away?
versets.commentaire: How should they believe now, who denied yesterday and hurled at the unseen baseless conjectures, “from afar”? The “far place” is the image of their former blindness: they aimed at the unseen blindly, as one shoots an arrow toward a target one cannot see. The faith of the last hour comes from too distant a place because they had themselves stood far from the Truth when it was offered.

mots.racine: ق ذ ف / غ ي ب
mots.sens: they hurl conjectures at the unseen
mots.def: to aim at the unknown without knowledge.

mots.racine: ك و ن / ب ع د

versets.ar: وَحِيلَ بَيْنَهُمْ وَبَيْنَ مَا يَشْتَهُونَ كَمَا فُعِلَ بِأَشْيَاعِهِم مِّن قَبْلُ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا فِي شَكٍّ مُّرِيبٍ
versets.tr: wa-ḥīla baynahum wa-bayna mā yashtahūna kamā fuʿila bi-ashyāʿihim min qablu innahum kānū fī shakkin murībin
versets.trad: And a barrier is set between them and what they desire, as was done with their like before. Indeed, they were in disquieting doubt.
versets.commentaire: The sūra's seal: a barrier rises between them and what they crave — repentance. Qushayrī: it is repentance they desire at the end, and the time has passed; they would appease the adversary, but shame prevents them from speaking at that hour; their tongue is knotted and paralyzed, and they can no longer express what is in their heart; they would wish that between them and what they committed a great distance were set; they would wish to obey, but strength no longer seconds them; they would wish, before leaving this world, for one more breath — and it does not come to pass.

mots.sens: a barrier is set between them

mots.racine: ش ك ك / ر ي ب
